page: Microsoft Azure D4d_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DDv4 server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The instance features 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, resulting in 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Additionally, the server includes 154 GB of local storage, while offering zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations indicate moderate single-core performance alongside weaker multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. Single-core tests, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, show average performance, with single-threaded gzip compression reaching 25.16 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 9.9 GB/sec. However, multi-core scaling is relatively weak, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 2921.0 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 18.48 GB/sec, which falls into the bottom 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with uncached large-block read speeds registering at a poor 22.2 GB/sec and write speeds at 18.17 GB/sec.

Given its balanced resource profile and dedicated CPU allocation, the D4d_v4 is qualitatively cost-effective for workloads requiring a steady baseline of compute and memory without extreme throughput demands. The presence of 154 GB of local storage supports applications that benefit from low-latency scratch space. However, the weak multi-core scaling and low memory bandwidth make it less suitable for intensive database operations, high-throughput web serving, or complex software compilation. It is best utilized for general-purpose workloads, basic text processing, light single-threaded applications, and development environments.
description: Microsoft Azure D4d_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DDv4 family, configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz, this x86_64 instance includes 154 GB of local storage and operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Benchmark data reveals average single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1332.0 and stable single-threaded compression. However, multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth are relatively weak, with uncached large-block reads limited to 22.2 GB/sec. The instance offers a balanced resource profile suitable for general-purpose applications, light text processing, and development environments that require dedicated CPU resources and local scratch space, though it is less optimal for memory-intensive or highly parallelized workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4d_v4 is a general-purpose VM featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 154 GB local storage, offering balanced single-core performance for standard workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4d_v4 is a general-purpose VM with 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 154 GB local storage, managed via Hyper-V.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and integrated local storage for standard enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB RAM
Includes 154 GB of local storage for low-latency scratch space
Average single-core performance for text processing and single-threaded tasks
Weaker multi-core scaling and constrained memory bandwidth under heavy loads
Best suited for general-purpose applications, development, and light workloads
